skill.name: tophub-trends
skill.description: Fetches TopHub hot-list data and asks Claude to generate content topic ideas and Markdown reports.
skill.summary: Analyze TopHub trending topics for content planning and topic selection.
skill.author: wangdefou-dev

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution and path traversal are mostly false positives caused by Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals. Real risks remain because the skill loads a local .env file, uses an Anthropic API key, fetches external TopHub data, sends trend text to Claude, and writes reports to disk. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: External Data Fetch and AI Service Transfer
medium_findings.description: The skill fetches live TopHub data and sends the top trend list to the Anthropic API for analysis. This is core functionality, but users should know that scraped titles, links, and popularity labels leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls and prompt construction are directly visible. The behavior appears intentional and legitimate, but it still transfers data to external services.

medium_findings.title: Environment File and API Key Access
medium_findings.description: The script loads a parent .env file, reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and supports ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. The API key is not logged, but secret handling and a configurable API endpoint require user trust.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dotenv loading and environment variable reads are explicit. No evidence shows credential theft, but the code depends on sensitive local configuration.

medium_findings.title: Local Report and Raw Data Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill creates an outputs/trends directory and writes raw trend data plus a Markdown analysis report. Paths are derived from the project root and timestamps, so this is expected behavior with limited scope.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are direct and scoped to generated output files. The risk is moderate because marketplace users may not expect local file creation.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Web Content Included in Prompt
medium_findings.description: TopHub titles, sources, hotness values, and links are inserted into the Claude prompt without explicit instructions to treat them as untrusted data. A manipulated trend item could influence the generated report.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data-to-prompt flow is clear, but there is no tool-calling or command execution based on model output. The likely impact is report manipulation, not system compromise.

low_findings.title: Shell Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported external command hits are Markdown code fences, logging statements, and TypeScript template literals. No evidence found of child_process, eval, shell invocation, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain documentation or string interpolation, not shell execution APIs. Static matching confused backticks with command execution syntax.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Finding Is Fixed-Path Project Resolution
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts point to path.resolve(__dirname, '../../'), which computes a fixed project root. No evidence found that user input controls this path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path segment is hardcoded and then used for expected .env and output locations. There is no dynamic path input in the reviewed code.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and high-entropy findings point to SKILL.md frontmatter and Chinese text. No evidence found of cryptographic code, encoded payloads, or hidden binary content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible metadata and a natural-language description. The scanner likely misclassified non-English text and the word shape in the description.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network, Secret, and Filesystem Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines external network requests, API-key usage, and local file writes. The combination is explainable for this use case, but it warrants marketplace disclosure and review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combined behavior is present in the code. Context supports a legitimate workflow, so it is not treated as confirmed malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configurable Anthropic Base URL
dangerous_patterns.description: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL can change the API endpoint used with the Anthropic client. This can be useful for proxies, but users should only set it to trusted endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint configuration is explicit. The risk depends on local configuration, not on a hardcoded malicious endpoint.

content.user_title: Analyze TopHub Trends for Content Ideas
content.value_statement: Creators need a fast way to turn daily hot lists into useful topic ideas. This skill fetches TopHub trends, asks Claude to analyze traffic potential, and saves a Markdown report.
actual_capabilities: Fetches current TopHub hot-list entries from https://tophub.today/hot.
Extracts rank, title, source, hotness label, and link for each trend item.
Sends the top 30 items to Claude for content strategy analysis.
Generates five content angle suggestions in Markdown format.
Saves raw trend data and the analysis report under outputs/trends.
Supports a mock mode for testing without calling the Anthropic API.
limitations: Requires Node.js dependencies and ts-node to run the TypeScript script.
Requires a valid Anthropic API key unless MOCK_MODE is enabled.
Depends on the current TopHub page structure and availability.
Generated recommendations need editorial review before publication.

use_cases.title: Plan Daily Creator Topics
use_cases.description: Review current TopHub topics and receive suggested angles for short videos, posts, or newsletters.

use_cases.title: Support Editorial Meetings
use_cases.description: Generate a quick trend brief before deciding which stories deserve deeper coverage.

use_cases.title: Track Market Attention
use_cases.description: Save raw hot-list data and analysis reports for later comparison across dates.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Trend Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup with normal API access
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the TopHub trends skill to fetch the latest hot list and create a Markdown report with topic suggestions.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Safer Content Angles
prompt_templates.scenario: Creator wants ideas but wants to avoid sensitive framing
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the latest TopHub trends and suggest content angles that avoid misleading claims, harassment, and unverified speculation.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Topics by Audience Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Team has a known audience niche
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch TopHub trends and rank the best opportunities for a productivity-focused audience. Explain why each topic fits or does not fit.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Editorial Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a publishing team
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the TopHub trend analysis, then produce an editorial brief with priority topics, risks, source checks, and recommended publishing formats.

output_examples.input: Fetch the latest TopHub trends and analyze the top items.
output_examples.output: A Markdown report listing high-potential topics, proposed content angles, and short explanations of why each angle may attract attention.

output_examples.input: Use mock mode to test the workflow.
output_examples.output: A sample Markdown report is saved locally without calling the Anthropic API.

output_examples.input: Save the source trend data for later review.
output_examples.output: A timestamped raw data file and a matching analysis report are written to the trends output folder.

best_practices: Review generated angles for accuracy, fairness, and brand fit before publishing.
Use a trusted Anthropic endpoint and protect the .env file that stores the API key.
Check TopHub source links before relying on any trend as factual evidence.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated trend analysis without verifying the underlying sources.
Do not store production API keys in shared repositories or public workspaces.
Do not assume every viral topic is suitable for your audience or brand.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It fetches TopHub hot-list data, asks Claude to analyze trend potential, and saves a Markdown topic report.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unless MOCK_MODE is set to true for testing.

faq.question: Where are results saved?
faq.answer: The script writes raw data and analysis reports to outputs/trends under the project root.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No direct shell execution was found in the reviewed TypeScript code. The documentation shows an npx command for users to run.

faq.question: Can it use a custom Anthropic endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes. ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL can configure the client endpoint, so users should only use trusted endpoints.

faq.question: Is the output ready to publish?
faq.answer: No. The report should be treated as research support and reviewed for accuracy, tone, and source quality.
